case_id: NACA 4415 airfoil with 10-degree trailing edge flap deflection at 75% chord

pi_items.source: NACA 4-digit airfoil designation system: 4 = 4% maximum camber, 4 = 40% chord position of camber, 15 = 15% maximum thickness. Verified against NACA airfoil standards and NACA 4-digit generator documentation.
Verifier code line 17 (HINGE_X = 0.75) and QBlade documentation showing trailing edge flap hinge specification in fractional chord units. 75% chord is a standard position for trailing edge flaps.

Verifier code line 18 (TARGET_ANGLE_DEG = 10.0) and verifier acceptance criteria (lines 189-196) expecting 5-15 degree range with 10 degrees as ideal. Task description specifies '10 degrees downward (positive)'.
Task description line 1, setup_task.sh lines 13-14 (cleanup), export_result.sh lines 12-28 (file checking), verifier.py line 31 (default path reading). Path is consistent across all task files.

Task description line 2, setup_task.sh line 14 (cleanup), export_result.sh lines 13-42 (file checking), verifier.py line 32 (default path reading). File naming 'flap10' indicates 10-degree deflection. Path is consistent across all task files.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires generating a NACA 4415 airfoil (4% camber at 40% chord, 15% thickness) using QBlade, then applying a 10-degree trailing edge flap deflection at 75% chord position, and exporting both the original and modified coordinates to .dat files. NACA 4415 is a real, well-documented airfoil standard. QBlade is verified open-source wind turbine design software with built-in NACA generator and trailing edge flap modification capability. All metadata parameters (hinge position 0.75, deflection angle 10.0, NACA designation 4415) are verified against software documentation and verifier code constants.
